warnings: Hole pattern vertical spacing: dimensions .375 (bottom offset), 2.750, and 5.500 appear to be a baseline dimension stack from the bottom edge (0.375, 3.125=0.375+2.750, 5.875=0.375+2.750+2.750=5.875, matching 5.500 as span between first and last hole approx +0.375 offset) - interpreted as bottom offset .375, then two 2.750 spacings up to 5.875; verify exact stack interpretation against original drawing since 5.500 target doesn't perfectly match 5.875.

overview_analysis.overall_shape_summary: A flat rectangular steel plate (cover), 11.00 x 6.25 x .105 (12 GA) thick, with a rectangular notch/cutout centered along one long edge, six .218 dia through-holes arranged in two rows of three (bolt pattern), and a strip of weather-stripping applied to one edge as shown in the edge (side) view.

views_detected.description: Top/plan view of the flat plate showing overall length 11.00, width 6.25, a rectangular notch cut into the top edge, and six through-holes located near the corners/edges with X-Y offset dimensioning.
Thin edge-on (side) view of the plate showing its .105 (12 GA) thickness and a small step/tab feature at one end, with two inspection balloons pointing to the thickness and length.
Title block with part name COVER, part number 158-C, model VERT. 90, material and drafting standard notes.

Bill of materials table listing Item 1 (Plate, 1018 STL 12GA, size .105x6.25x11.00) and Item 2 (Weather Stripping, McMaster-Carr #1117A11 or equiv, sponge rubber adhesive-back, 5/32 THK x 1/2 WIDE, qty 12.5 IN).

symmetry.notes: Hole locations are dimensioned using rectangular X/Y offsets (1.56, 1.62, 5.500, 2.750, .375, 10.500, .25) rather than polar/angular dimensions, which is evidence AGAINST a rotational (polar) bolt pattern. The plate appears to have possible left-right mirror symmetry about its horizontal centerline (two rows of 3 holes), but the notch is offset toward the left side (1.56 from left edge), breaking any left-right mirror symmetry of the overall part.

flags_contributed.human_note: CROSS-VIEW CONFLICT (front, edge_side): The front view clearly shows six hole locations (center-cross marks) matching the '.218 DR. THRU 6-HLS.' callout, so hole count is confirmed and consistent — no conflict here. However, the notch feature (1.56/1.62/1.88 dims) shown in the front view has no corresponding representation or cross-check in the edge/side view, making it impossible to confirm the notch's depth relative to plate thickness or verify it is a through-cut versus a partial depth feature from the edge view alone. Recommendation: Verify from full-size front view crop that notch is a through-cutout (open to the plate edge) and confirm no additional profile view was omitted that would show the notch depth through the thickness. [request-full-sheet: this looks like a cropped/off-sheet region — upload the complete sheet rather than re-running.]

CROSS-VIEW CONFLICT (front, edge_side): The edge/side view shows a small tab or step feature at one end of the plate that does not have an obvious corresponding dimension or feature call-out in the front (plan) view. Recommendation: Check enlarged crop of edge view to determine if this step is a drafting artifact (fold line, weld line) or an actual formed tab; cross-check against front view edge for any unlabeled break line. [request-full-sheet: this looks like a cropped/off-sheet region — upload the complete sheet rather than re-running.]

HOLE-COUNT DISAGREEMENT: the sheet's global callout "UNLESS OTHERWISE SPECIFIED: THIRD ANGLE PROJECTION, DIMENSIONS IN INCHES, 2 PLACE DIM ±.02, 3 PLACE DIM ±.010, ANGULAR DIM ±, SCALE 1"=1"" states 2 instance(s), but the per-view extraction captured hole group(s) of [6] (total 6). resolution.plain_english: All dimensions confirmed by arithmetic chain or explicit callout. Model is complete and buildable as-is.
